compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Root cause (proven, joe-debug): NOT a signing-on-BLOCK code defect. plan_review/__init__.py:172-189 signs ONLY when verdict=='PASS'; on BLOCK the else-branch emits signature={signed:False, reason:'BLOCK'} — the object is ALWAYS present (non-null) but .signed is False. The claim gate (attest.py:claim_gate_check) verifies a real HMAC signature EVENT on the ticket via signing.verify_signature; sign_manifest is never called on BLOCK, so no event is written and the gate denies. Verified no other path (drift_refresh, orchestrator:543) signs on non-PASS. The reporter's 'signed=True' came from external batch tooling doing a PRESENCE/truthiness check on the always-present signature object instead of reading signature.signed. => Real bug is contract/docs clarity, not behavior. Fix: document the exact signature contract in docs/plan-review-gate.md + add a regression test pinning BLOCK->signed:False (no HMAC event, claim gate denies) and PASS->signed:True.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Resolved in PR #93 (commit d755bfe4e). NOT a code defect: review_plan signs only a genuine non-blocking PASS; BLOCK/INDETERMINATE/exempt emit signature={signed:false, reason:<verdict>} and write NO HMAC event, so the claim gate can never be satisfied by a BLOCK (AC#2 structurally met). The footgun was a presence/truthiness check on the always-present signature object instead of reading signature.signed. Fix: documented the exact contract in docs/plan-review-gate.md (when a signature is emitted + what it guarantees, with an explicit 'read .signed, not presence' callout), AC#1/#3. Regression tests added (AC#4): BLOCK->signed:false + no event + claim denied; PASS->signed:true; mutation-checked (signing-on-BLOCK turns them RED).

compiled_state.description:
`rebar review-plan` reports `signature` present (the JSON `signature` field is non-null, and
batch tooling reads `signed=True`) even when the **verdict is BLOCK**. The documented contract
is that it "signs a plan-review attestation on a **non-blocking PASS**" — so a BLOCK verdict
should NOT carry a passing signed attestation. Either the signing-on-BLOCK is a bug, or the
`signature` field means something other than "passing attestation"; either way it is misleading,
because the signature is meant to be the trustworthy proof-of-PASS that downstream gates (the
claim gate) and CI rely on. A consumer that checks "is there a signature?" to decide
claimability would be misled by a signed BLOCK.

## Reproduction Steps
1. Author a ticket whose plan trips a blocking criterion (or use any ticket the four-pass gate
   BLOCKs).
2. Run `rebar review-plan <id> --output json` (default source = attested, signing on).
3. Inspect the result JSON: `verdict` and `signature`.

## Expected
- On `verdict == BLOCK`, no passing attestation is signed — `signature` is null/absent (or the
  field is unambiguously labeled as a non-passing attestation), so "has signature" reliably
  means "passed".

## Actual
- Observed repeatedly this session: tickets with `verdict=BLOCK` returned `signature` populated
  (`signed=True`). Concretely, Round-2/3/4 runs on the S4/S6/S7 d251 children showed e.g.
  `s6: BLOCK block=6 ... signed=True` and `s4: BLOCK block=2 ... signed=True` — a signature on a
  BLOCK.

## Notes
- Inferred from the JSON `signature` field, not the signing internals — possible the field is
  populated-but-not-a-passing-attestation; that distinction is exactly what's unclear and should
  be made explicit. Surfaced while dogfooding the plan-review gate on epic d251 (alto-fruit-punch).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `rebar review-plan <id>` on a BLOCK verdict does NOT emit a passing signed attestation
      (either `signature` is null/absent on BLOCK, or the field/type is explicitly distinct from
      a passing attestation and documented as such).
- [ ] `verify_signature` / the claim gate cannot be satisfied by an attestation produced on a
      BLOCK verdict (a BLOCKed plan is never claimable on the strength of its signature).
- [ ] The behavior is documented (docs/plan-review-gate.md): exactly when a signature is emitted
      and what its presence guarantees.
- [ ] A regression test asserts: BLOCK verdict -> no passing signature; PASS verdict -> signature present.

file_impact.reason: document signature contract (signed bool vs presence)

compiled_state.title: plan-review: signed attestation emitted on BLOCK verdict (signature should mean PASS)
